contents.markdown:
# Video: 00:00.733 => 00:43.233
Width: 1080
Height: 608

Transcript
```
WEBVTT

00:01.360 --> 00:06.640
<Speaker 1>When it comes to the neural TTS, in order to get a good voice, it's better to have good data.

00:07.120 --> 00:13.320
<Speaker 2>To achieve that, we build a universal TTS model based on 3,000 hours of data.

00:13.440 --> 00:23.680
<Speaker 1>We actually accumulated tons of the data so that this universal model is able to capture the nuance of the audio and generate a more natural voice for the algorithm.

00:24.040 --> 00:29.120
<Speaker 3>What we liked about cognitive services offerings were that they had a much higher fidelity.

00:29.600 --> 00:32.880
<Speaker 3>And they sounded a lot more like an actual human voice.

00:33.680 --> 00:37.200
<Speaker 4>Orlando ground 9555 requesting the end of pushback.

00:38.680 --> 00:41.280
<Speaker 4>9555 request to end pushback received.
```

Summary.valueString: The video opens with a scenic aerial view of an island and a small plane flying, introducing a collaboration between Flight Simulator and Microsoft Azure AI. It then transitions to a speaker discussing the importance of high-quality data for neural text-to-speech (TTS) technology, mentioning the creation of a universal TTS model trained on 3,000 hours of data to capture audio nuances and produce natural-sounding voices. Visuals include audio waveforms and shots of data centers, emphasizing the technology's backend. The speaker praises cognitive services for their high fidelity and human-like voice quality. The segment concludes with realistic flight simulation visuals, including planes taxiing and preparing for takeoff, highlighting the integration of advanced AI voice technology in the simulation environment.

transcriptPhrases.text: When it comes to the neural TTS, in order to get a good voice, it's better to have good data.

transcriptPhrases.speaker: Speaker 2
transcriptPhrases.text: To achieve that, we build a universal TTS model based on 3,000 hours of data.

transcriptPhrases.speaker: Speaker 1
transcriptPhrases.text: We actually accumulated tons of the data so that this universal model is able to capture the nuance of the audio and generate a more natural voice for the algorithm.

transcriptPhrases.speaker: Speaker 3
transcriptPhrases.text: What we liked about cognitive services offerings were that they had a much higher fidelity.

transcriptPhrases.speaker: Speaker 3
transcriptPhrases.text: And they sounded a lot more like an actual human voice.

transcriptPhrases.speaker: Speaker 4
transcriptPhrases.text: Orlando ground 9555 requesting the end of pushback.

transcriptPhrases.text: 9555 request to end pushback received.

Summary.valueString: The video opens with a collaboration logo between Flight Simulator and Microsoft Azure AI, followed by a discussion on neural text-to-speech (TTS) technology. A speaker explains the importance of high-quality data for good voice synthesis and describes building a universal TTS model trained on 3,000 hours of data to capture audio nuances and generate natural-sounding voices. Visuals include sound waveforms and data centers, emphasizing the technology's scale and sophistication.

transcriptPhrases.text: To achieve that, we build a universal TTS model based on 3,000 hours of data.

transcriptPhrases.speaker: Speaker 1
transcriptPhrases.text: We actually accumulated tons of the data so that this universal model is able to capture the nuance of the audio and generate a more natural voice for the algorithm.

Summary.valueString: The video transitions to showcasing the application of the TTS technology in a flight simulation environment. It features scenic aerial views of landscapes and castles, highlighting the realistic graphics of the Flight Simulator. The narration continues to emphasize the high fidelity and human-like quality of the voices generated by the cognitive services.

Summary.valueString: The video opens with a collaboration announcement between Flight Simulator and Microsoft Azure AI, showcasing a small plane flying over a tropical island. It then transitions to a discussion about neural text-to-speech (TTS) technology, emphasizing the importance of high-quality data and the development of a universal TTS model trained on 3,000 hours of data to capture audio nuances and produce natural-sounding voices. Visuals include audio waveform displays and shots of data centers and server farms, highlighting the technological infrastructure behind the TTS system. The segment concludes with a demonstration of the TTS's high fidelity, comparing it to human voice quality, and ends with a scene of an airplane on the tarmac, accompanied by air traffic control communications.
